daily_transits.source: Windward AI (April 8 Maritime Intelligence Daily) / Bloomberg / Kpler

cross_validation.note: April 7: 11 vessels (4 inbound sanctioned tankers + 7 outbound via IRGC Northern Corridor); April 8 midday: 5 bulk carriers outbound only, traffic unchanged post-ceasefire
Bloomberg: 'just three ships observed leaving on Wednesday (Apr 8)'; Kpler: only 5 bulk carriers crossed on Wednesday, no oil/gas tankers; pre-war baseline ~135/day
Week of Mar 30–Apr 5 had 72 transits total (busiest since war began), ~90% below normal; 80% Iran-connected, 13% China-owned

milestones.bluechip_operators_returning: no — Maersk issued cautious statement: 'not making any changes to specific services'; Windward confirms 'No blue-chip operators present, major oil majors absent'
milestones.war_risk_premium_status: peak — Lloyd's List reports 2.5% standard and up to 5% for US/UK/Israeli-nexus vessels; hull war cover at 1%+ of ship value; P&I clubs still partially withdrawn; no measurable decline post-ceasefire
milestones.negotiation_progress: Pakistan-mediated 2-week ceasefire announced April 7–8; US-Iran direct talks scheduled Islamabad April 10; US team led by VP Vance/Witkoff/Kushner; ceasefire fragile — Iran parliament speaker alleges violations, Lebanese operations ongoing; Iran's 10-point proposal accepted as 'workable basis'; nuclear enrichment remains key sticking point
milestones.iran_toll_status: negotiating — Iran reportedly plans crypto toll demands (per CNBC/Windward); Iran's 10-point proposal includes 'dominance and oversight' of Hormuz; White House demands opening 'without limitation, including tolls'

market_signals.defense_stocks_note: Apr 9: RTX +2.40% ($202.68), NOC -0.38% ($687.85), LMT -1.60% ($627.70) — defense stocks broadly lower on ceasefire relief rally reversing; RTX outperforms on missile/interceptor demand; LMT down as war-pause reduces near-term strike contract urgency; defense sector still up ~38% YTD

reports.trading_signal: Ceasefire announced April 7–8 triggered 15%+ oil price crash (Brent ~$93–96), but Apr 9 oil rebounded +4–7% as market recognized Hormuz remains IRGC-controlled and physically closed to mainstream shipping. Iran confirmed ships must coordinate with IRGC; ADNOC CEO stated strait 'has not opened to ship traffic.' Goldman Sachs flagged $100+ Brent if closure persists another month. Ceasefire is fragile (Vance: 'ceasefires are always messy'); Israel-Lebanon operations ongoing complicate Iran buy-in. Islamabad talks (Apr 10) are the next critical catalyst.
reports.verdict: 霍尔木兹海峡名义停火、实质仍受伊斯兰革命卫队管控，每日通航量仅约5艘（正常135艘的3.7%），零蓝筹船公司复航，战争险保费维持峰值，恢复评分1/100，属于'受监管暂停'而非真正重开。
key_news: 2026-04-07/08 美伊巴基斯坦斡旋下达成两周停火，伊朗外长宣布霍尔木兹可通行但须'与伊朗武装力量协调'，IRGC北部走廊管控不变
彭博/Kpler数据：4月8日仅3-5艘船（全为散货轮）通过，零油轮/LNG船，零蓝筹运营商；超800艘船只（含近800艘油轮/货船）仍困于海湾内等候
停火脆弱性高：伊朗议会议长指美国违反停火协议（黎巴嫩打击持续），ADNOC CEO 4月9日确认海峡'未对船舶交通开放'，油价当日反弹逾6%至WTI$100+；伊斯兰堡谈判(4月10日)为下一关键节点
